description: Chausath Khamba located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh.

description: Kinari Bazaar located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh.

description: Gyarah Sidi located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh.

name: Taj Nature Walk
description: Taj Nature Walk located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh.

name: Brijwasi Mithai Wala
description: Iconic sweet shop famous for Mathura Peda and traditional Braj sweets since 1950s.

name: Zaika Restaurant
description: Popular multi-cuisine restaurant in Mathura offering North Indian, Chinese, and South Indian food.

name: Mathura Chaat Bhandar
description: Famous street food stall known for Aloo Tikki, Kachori, and Chaat in the heart of Mathura.

name: Govinda's Restaurant (ISKCON)
description: Pure vegetarian sattvic restaurant inside ISKCON Vrindavan serving prasadam meals.

name: Vrindavan Lassi Corner
description: Famous lassi shop near Bankey Bihari Temple serving thick malai lassi and rabri.

name: MVT (Madhu Van) Restaurant
description: Popular vegetarian restaurant in Vrindavan with AC dining and thali meals.

name: Panchhi Petha Store
description: Agra's most famous petha shop since 1956. A must-visit for the iconic Agra Petha sweet.

name: Mama Chicken
description: Popular non-veg restaurant in Agra known for butter chicken and biryani.

name: Deviram Sweets & Namkeen
description: Traditional sweet and namkeen shop in Agra famous for bedai-jalebi breakfast.

name: Gokul Dham Bhojanalaya
description: Simple vegetarian bhojanshala serving traditional Braj food near Gokul temples.

name: Govardhan Annakut Bhog
description: Famous prasad and food stall at Govardhan parikrama route offering sattvic bhog thali.

name: ISKCON Guesthouse Vrindavan
description: Clean and affordable guesthouse inside ISKCON campus with spiritual atmosphere.

name: Hotel Sheela Agra
description: Budget-friendly hotel near Taj Mahal with rooftop views and clean rooms.

location.address: Near East Gate, Taj Mahal, Agra

name: Mathura Dharamshala
description: Affordable dharamshala near Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi with basic facilities for pilgrims.

name: The Gateway Hotel Agra
description: Premium Taj Group hotel in Agra with pool, spa, and fine dining restaurant.

name: Nidhivan Sarovar Portico
description: Comfortable mid-range hotel in Vrindavan with modern amenities and spiritual ambiance.

name: Holi Gate Market Mathura
description: Bustling market near Holi Gate famous for religious items, clothes, sweets, and local handicrafts.

name: Sadar Bazaar Agra
description: Main commercial market of Agra with marble crafts, leather goods, and Mughlai items.

name: Baba Doodh Wala
description: Legendary milk and rabri shop in Vrindavan famous for fresh malai and kulfi.

name: Brijwasi Royal Restaurant
description: Upscale vegetarian restaurant in Mathura with AC, thali meals, and Braj specialties.

name: Pinch of Spice Agra
description: Popular family restaurant in Agra with North Indian, Mughlai, and Continental cuisine.

name: Shri Krishna Janmasthan Temple
description: The birthplace of Lord Krishna, one of the holiest sites in Hinduism. The temple complex includes the prison cell where Krishna was born.

name: Dwarkadhish Temple
description: A magnificent temple dedicated to Lord Krishna as the King of Dwarka. Famous for its intricate carvings and Rajasthani architecture.

name: Vishram Ghat
description: The most sacred ghat on the Yamuna in Mathura, where Lord Krishna is said to have rested after slaying Kansa. Evening aarti is spectacular.

name: Potara Kund
description: Ancient stepped tank near Krishna Janmasthan where baby Krishna's clothes were washed. A serene architectural marvel.

name: Government Museum Mathura
description: One of India's finest museums with sculptures from the Kushan and Gupta periods. Houses exquisite Mathura school of art pieces.

name: Kusum Sarovar
description: A beautiful stepped water tank surrounded by ancient cenotaphs. Associated with Radha's collection of flowers for Krishna.

name: Rangji Temple
description: A unique temple blending South Indian and Mughal architectural styles. Features a stunning 50-foot tall gopuram.

name: Kans Qila (Kansa Fort)
description: An ancient fort overlooking the Yamuna, believed to be the palace of the tyrant king Kansa. Offers panoramic views of the river.

name: Gita Mandir
description: Modern temple with the entire Bhagavad Gita inscribed on its walls. Also houses a depiction of all 24 avatars of Vishnu.

name: Brijwasi Mithai Wala
description: Iconic Mathura sweet shop famous for its pedas, kachori, and lassi. A must-visit for authentic Braj cuisine.

name: Mathura Chaat Gali
description: Famous street food lane near Vishram Ghat serving Mathura's legendary chaat, kachori, jalebi, and dahi vada.

name: Hotel Brijwasi Royal
description: Popular mid-range hotel near the city center with AC rooms, restaurant, and parking. Good base for Mathura-Vrindavan tour.

name: Yamuna Aarti Point
description: The main aarti point on the Yamuna at Vishram Ghat. The evening aarti with hundreds of diyas floating is mesmerizing.

name: Dauji Temple
description: Ancient temple dedicated to Balarama (Krishna's elder brother). Famous for the Huranga Holi celebration.

name: Keshav Dev Temple
description: Temple within the Krishna Janmasthan complex, built on the original site of Krishna's birth. Deeply spiritual atmosphere.

name: Banke Bihari Temple
description: The most popular temple in Vrindavan, famous for the enchanting deity of Krishna in his tribhangi pose. Known for its unique darshan style.

name: ISKCON Vrindavan (Krishna Balaram Mandir)
description: The international headquarters of ISKCON in Vrindavan. Beautiful marble temple with devotional programs throughout the day.

name: Prem Mandir
description: A stunning white marble temple illuminated at night. Depicts scenes of Krishna's leelas with Radha. Musical fountain show in evening.

name: Nidhivan
description: Sacred grove where Krishna is believed to perform Raas Leela every night. The trees grow in pairs and the tulsi plants remain green year-round.

name: Radha Raman Temple
description: A 500-year-old temple housing a self-manifested deity of Krishna. One of the most important Vaishnavite temples in Vrindavan.

name: Radha Vallabh Temple
description: One of the seven original temples of Vrindavan, dedicated to Radha as the supreme deity. Unique because the idol shows only Radha.

name: Seva Kunj
description: A sacred garden where Krishna is said to have served Radha. The garden closes at sunset as it's believed divine leelas occur at night.

name: Kesi Ghat
description: An ancient ghat on the Yamuna where Krishna killed the demon horse Kesi. Beautiful for morning walks and evening aarti.

name: Rangaji Temple Vrindavan
description: A grand temple in South Indian Dravidian style with a 60-foot tall gopuram. Dedicated to Lord Ranganatha (Vishnu).

name: Vrindavan Chandrodaya Mandir
description: An under-construction mega temple complex that will be one of the tallest temples in the world at 210 meters.

name: MVT (Meera Vihar Temple) Guest House
description: Popular spiritual stay run by ISKCON devotees. Clean, vegetarian food, and close to all major temples.

name: Govind Dham Restaurant
description: Pure vegetarian restaurant serving authentic North Indian thali, paneer dishes, and fresh rotis. Near Banke Bihari Temple.

name: Brajwasi Restaurant
description: Popular eatery known for its puri-sabzi, lassi, and South Indian dosa. Budget-friendly and quick service.

name: Loi Bazaar
description: Vibrant market near Banke Bihari Temple selling tulsi malas, Krishna idols, bangles, and devotional items.

name: Yamuna Boat Ride Vrindavan
description: Scenic boat ride on the Yamuna river offering views of ghats and temples. Especially beautiful at sunset.

name: Radha Rani Temple (Shriji Temple)
description: The main temple atop Barsana hill dedicated to Radha Rani. Offers breathtaking panoramic views of the entire Braj region.

name: Lathmar Holi Ground
description: The famous site where Barsana's Lathmar Holi is celebrated. Women beat men with sticks in this unique festival tradition.

name: Pili Pokhar
description: Sacred yellow pond at the base of Barsana hill. Legend says Radha used to play here with her friends.

name: Rangibihari Temple
description: A beautiful temple at the foot of Barsana hill known for its colorful decorations and peaceful atmosphere.

name: Maan Garh
description: Hill fort ruins where Radha would pretend to be angry (maan) with Krishna. Offers spectacular views at sunset.

name: Daan Bihari Temple
description: Temple marking the spot where Krishna would tease the gopis and demand toll (daan) for passing. Part of the Radha-Krishna leela.

name: Bhanukhar (Vrishbhanu Kund)
description: The birthplace of Radha Rani, marked by a sacred kund (pond). King Vrishbhanu found baby Radha on a lotus here.

name: Barsana View Point
description: Scenic viewpoint atop the Barsana hills offering 360-degree views of the Braj countryside and green valleys.

name: Radha Rani Bhojanalaya
description: Simple vegetarian restaurant near the temple serving fresh rotis, sabzi, dal-chawal, and lassi. Affordable pilgrim food.

name: Priya Kund
description: Ancient sacred kund where Radha and her sakhis would bathe. Surrounded by old trees and a peaceful atmosphere.

name: Nand Bhawan
description: The house of Nanda Baba where Krishna grew up. Features beautiful paintings and murals depicting Krishna's childhood leelas.

name: Raman Reti
description: Sacred sandy ground where little Krishna played and rolled in the dust. Pilgrims apply this sand on their foreheads.

name: Brahmand Ghat
description: The ghat on the Yamuna where mother Yashoda saw the entire universe in baby Krishna's mouth. Deeply spiritual site.

name: Chintaharan Mahadev Temple
description: Ancient Shiva temple believed to remove all worries (chinta). Nanda Baba is said to have worshipped here for Krishna's protection.

name: Yashodanandan Temple
description: Temple celebrating the mother-son bond of Yashoda and Krishna. Features a beautiful idol of baby Krishna with butter.

name: Thakurani Ghat
description: A serene ghat on the Yamuna perfect for meditation. Less crowded than Mathura ghats with beautiful sunrise views.

name: Gokulnath Temple
description: A Pushti Marg temple established by Vallabhacharya. Important pilgrimage site for Vaishnavites.

name: Gokul Barrage
description: A scenic dam on the Yamuna offering beautiful views and a peaceful picnic spot. Good for families and photography.

name: Gokul Dham Bhojanalaya
description: Simple pilgrim restaurant serving fresh sattvic food — rotis, dal, sabzi, and buttermilk. Clean and affordable.

name: Chaurasi Khamba (84 Pillars)
description: An ancient structure with 84 pillars believed to be from the Mughal era. Each pillar has unique carvings.

name: Govardhan Hill (Giriraj)
description: The sacred hill that Lord Krishna lifted on his little finger for 7 days to protect the villagers from Indra's rain. Pilgrims do the 21 km parikrama.

name: Manasi Ganga
description: A sacred lake created by Krishna's mind (manas). Pilgrims do parikrama of this lake during festivals.

name: Daan Ghati Temple
description: The first major stop on the Govardhan Parikrama route. Krishna collected toll (daan) from gopis here.

name: Mukharvind Temple
description: Temple at the 'face' of Govardhan Hill. The hill's natural rock formation here resembles the face of Lord Krishna.

name: Radha Kund
description: The holiest bathing spot in Vrindavan-Braj region, even more sacred than the Ganges according to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

name: Shyam Kund
description: Sacred kund adjacent to Radha Kund. Created by Krishna to match Radha's kund. They are considered the eyes of Govardhan.

name: Harideva Temple
description: A grand red sandstone temple, one of the original temples of Braj. Features stunning Mughal-era architecture.

name: Jatipura
description: The starting point of the Govardhan Parikrama. Pilgrims begin their 21 km walk around the hill from here.

name: Govardhan Annakshetra
description: Free community kitchen (langar) near the parikrama route serving dal, roti, and rice to all pilgrims. Also has paid thali.

name: Punchari Ka Lautha
description: Ancient spot where Krishna's footprints are imprinted on the rock. Pilgrims come to touch and worship these marks.
